title: Stack-allocated sink arrays may overflow with large SINK_MAX config
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/app_interface/app_interface.c:281,313,345,375

Multiple functions allocate snapshot arrays on the stack using SINK_MAX:
  struct lichen_app_interface_sink snapshot[SINK_MAX];

Where SINK_MAX is defined as CONFIG_LICHEN_APP_INTERFACE_MAX_SUBSCRIBERS (line 18).

struct lichen_app_interface_sink is approximately 56 bytes (7 pointers on 64-bit). If CONFIG_LICHEN_APP_INTERFACE_MAX_SUBSCRIBERS is set too high, this causes:
- Stack overflow on embedded targets with limited stack
- Silent corruption on systems without stack guards

Functions affected:
- lichen_app_interface_emit_text() line 281
- lichen_app_interface_submit_text() line 313
- lichen_app_interface_emit_status() line 345
- lichen_app_interface_get_status() line 375
- lichen_app_interface_get_config() line 406
- lichen_app_interface_set_config() line 444

Consider:
1. Adding a static_assert or BUILD_ASSERT to limit SINK_MAX
2. Using static/heap allocation for the snapshot array
3. Documenting the stack size requirements
close_reason: Added BUILD_ASSERT(SINK_MAX <= 8) to prevent stack overflow in snapshot arrays; matches Kconfig range and codebase patterns; verified via build assertions in similar files
